remember: Recall the structure and function of the respiratory system in humans.
Identify the different types of breathing (inhalation and exhalation).
Explain the process of gas exchange in the lungs.
understand: Describe the role of oxygen and carbon dioxide in cellular respiration and photosynthesis.
Explain the mechanism of breathing and the regulation of breathing rate.
Understand the difference between external and internal respiration.
apply: Apply the concept of diffusion and osmosis to explain the process of gas exchange in the lungs.
Explain how the respiratory system adapts to changes in altitude and physical activity.
Use the Bohr effect to explain the role of carbon dioxide in hemoglobin function.
analyze: Analyze the factors that affect breathing rate and gas exchange in the lungs.
Compare the respiratory systems of humans and other animals.
Evaluate the impact of respiratory diseases on human health and well-being.

ncert_objectives: Describe the structure and function of the respiratory system in humans.
Explain the process of breathing and the regulation of breathing rate.
Describe the role of oxygen and carbon dioxide in cellular respiration and photosynthesis.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the process of gas exchange in the lungs.
Describe the structure and function of the alveoli and air sacs.
Explain the Bohr effect and its role in respiratory physiology.
